welcome.title: RevOps Assistant
welcome.subtitle: Revenue operations toolkit.
welcome.emptyState: First time? Set up your first project.

projectFile.importError: Could not read the project file
projectFile.checksumWarning: File integrity does not match; the contents may have been altered.
projectFile.collisionTitle: A project with this identifier already exists
projectFile.reconnectNotice: Reconnect HubSpot and Google Drive: credentials are not included in the file.

help.fallbackNotice: This tutorial isn't available in {{language}} yet; showing it in Spanish.

connectors.title: Connectors
connectors.hubspotDescription: Connect your HubSpot portal using a Private App Token.
connectors.googleDriveDescription: Connect a Google Drive folder as the source of truth for working files.
connectors.apiMcpDescription: Expose the app's capabilities to MCP clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor) through a local server.

mcp.title: Settings / API and MCP
mcp.regenerateBody: Regenerating the token invalidates the current one and ends every active MCP session. Continue?

fieldHelp.token: Local token that MCP clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor) use to authenticate against this server. Keep it private; regenerate it if it leaks.

hubspot.title: Connectors / HubSpot
hubspot.tokenHelp: The token is stored encrypted in the OS keychain and is never shown.

fieldHelp.token: HubSpot Private App token. It requires the CRM scopes (properties, objects and forms). You get it in Settings → Integrations → Private Apps. It is stored encrypted and never shown.
fieldHelp.environment: Choose the portal to connect: Production or Sandbox. Each environment stores its own token independently.

gdrive.title: Connectors / Google Drive
gdrive.conflicts: {{count}} file(s) in conflict: decide which version to keep.

credentials.title: Google Cloud credentials
credentials.clientSecretPlaceholder: Optional (only desktop apps that require it)
credentials.alreadySet: Set ({{preview}}) — type a new one to change it
credentials.alreadySetSecret: Set — type a new one to change it
credentials.help: Only the OAuth client ID is needed; the folder picker does not use an API key.
credentials.cleared: Credentials cleared; .env will be used if present.

fieldHelp.clientId: OAuth client ID of the project in Google Cloud Console (APIs & Services → Credentials). It is required to authorize access to Drive.
fieldHelp.clientSecret: OAuth client secret. Only needed for desktop apps that require it; leave it empty if it does not apply.

folderPicker.title: Select working folder
folderPicker.searchEmpty: No folders found with that name.

doc.noFolder: Select a Google Drive folder to use this feature.
doc.loadConfirmBody: This will replace the local state with the content of the Drive file. Continue?

dirtyGuard.title: Unsaved changes in Drive
dirtyGuard.body: You have changes that haven't been updated in the Drive file. Update it before leaving?
dirtyGuard.dontAsk: Don't ask again for this project

update.available: Version {{version}} available. It will install on restart.
update.downloaded: Update {{version}} ready. Restart to apply it.

sections.placeholder: This section will be completed in a later SPEC.

errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong on this screen
errorBoundary.body: A project value may be malformed. You can reload; if it persists, review this section's data.

properties.title: CRM / Properties

groupsModal.title: {{object}} groups
groupsModal.notEmptyHint: The group has properties: move or archive them before deleting it.
groupsModal.confirm1Body: You are about to request PERMANENT deletion of group "{{group}}" in HubSpot. This is destructive and affects production. Continue?
groupsModal.confirm2Body: Confirm deletion of group "{{group}}" again. It will only be applied if the group is empty.
groupsModal.toastRequested: Group deletion added to pending changes.

planningMap.noChanges: The map has no changes compared to the project.
planningMap.changelogIntro: Review the changes before creating drafts. Nothing is applied in HubSpot.
planningMap.needsActionTitle: Fields that need action (unresolved ambiguous type)

properties.empty: No properties yet. Sync with HubSpot or add one.
properties.noResults: No results with the current filters.

status.missingTooltip: Doesn't exist in HubSpot; convert it to «New» to create it

blocked.banner: {{count}} properties reference fields that don't exist in HubSpot and won't be created automatically.

convert.confirmBody: {{count}} properties will be converted to «New» mode so they can be created. Then sync to generate the changes.
convert.done: {{converted}} converted. Sync to create the properties.
convert.seededWarning: {{seeded}} without a prior definition: review them before applying.

panel.label: Label
panel.applyHint: This action writes to HubSpot. Choose the environment.

fieldHelp.name: Identifying name of the data origin.
fieldHelp.type: Nature of the origin: integration, migration, user or workflow.
fieldHelp.description: Description of the origin and which data it provides.
fieldHelp.objectName: HubSpot object affected by this origin.
fieldHelp.fields: Available fields of this origin object; they feed the «Field name» dropdown of the editable map.

fieldHelp.name: Descriptive name of the entry in the property map. It only identifies the entry locally; it is not sent to HubSpot.
fieldHelp.hubspotName: Internal (API) name of the property in HubSpot: lowercase, no spaces. Cannot be changed after creation.
fieldHelp.label: Visible label of the property in HubSpot (what users see).
fieldHelp.type: Data type of the property (text, number, date, enumeration, boolean…).
fieldHelp.fieldType: Field type: how the value is entered (text, dropdown, checkboxes, date…).
fieldHelp.hubspotMode: Indicates whether the property already exists in HubSpot or whether it is new and will be created when the change is applied.
fieldHelp.selectProperty: HubSpot property this entry points to. Filter by name to locate it.
fieldHelp.group: HubSpot property group where it will be placed. You can create a new one if it does not exist.
fieldHelp.origin: System or process the data comes from (integration, migration, user or workflow).
fieldHelp.sourceField: Name of the field in the origin that feeds this property.
fieldHelp.kind: Data type (number, text, boolean, limited options or memo); it determines the type in HubSpot.
fieldHelp.sourceObject: Origin object the source field belongs to.
fieldHelp.optionLabel: Visible text of the option for the end user.
fieldHelp.optionValue: Internal value stored in HubSpot for the option.
fieldHelp.notes: Internal notes about the mapping. They are not sent to HubSpot.

wizard.title: Add property
wizard.name: Property name
wizard.pasteSourceHint: One source value per line; the HubSpot value is mapped afterwards.
wizard.editExistingHint: You can edit the definition; the changes will be applied in HubSpot.
wizard.pasteHint: One value per line (value = label). For "label + value", set the separator.

advanced.hasUniqueValue: Unique value (cannot be changed after creation)

fieldHelp.description: Description of the property that will be shown in HubSpot.
fieldHelp.numberDisplayHint: How the number is presented in HubSpot (unformatted, currency, percentage, duration, etc.).
fieldHelp.showCurrencySymbol: Shows the currency symbol next to the value when the format is monetary.
fieldHelp.currencyPropertyName: Related currency property that determines the currency to display.
fieldHelp.textDisplayHint: Text format in HubSpot (plain, multi-line, email, phone, etc.).
fieldHelp.calculationFormula: Calculation formula using the HubSpot API syntax (e.g. closed - started).
fieldHelp.dataSensitivity: Sensitivity level of the data; it affects its handling and visibility in HubSpot.
fieldHelp.hasUniqueValue: Forces the value to be unique across records. It cannot be changed after the property is created.
fieldHelp.formField: Lets the property be used as a field in HubSpot forms and in bots/chatflows. If left unchecked, HubSpot applies its default value.

properties.deleteEntryBody: This removes the property entry from the local map. This action cannot be undone. Continue?
properties.deleteOriginBody: This removes the data origin and its mappings. This action cannot be undone. Continue?

customObjects.title: CRM / Custom objects
customObjects.empty: No custom objects yet. Create one or sync with HubSpot.

panel.title: Object detail
panel.name: Internal name
panel.archiveWarning: Archiving requires deleting the object's records and properties in HubSpot first.

wizard.title: Create custom object
wizard.name: Internal name
wizard.nameHelp: Lowercase letters, numbers and underscores only; must start with a letter. Cannot be changed later.

fieldHelp.name: Internal name of the object: lowercase letters, numbers and underscores only; must start with a letter. Cannot be changed after it is created.
fieldHelp.singular: Singular label the user will see (e.g. "Order").
fieldHelp.plural: Plural label the user will see (e.g. "Orders").
fieldHelp.description: Description of the custom object and its purpose.
fieldHelp.propName: Internal name of the property: lowercase letters, numbers and underscores.
fieldHelp.propFieldType: Field type used to enter the data in HubSpot.
fieldHelp.unique: The property must have a unique value across records.
fieldHelp.primary: Main property that identifies and displays each record of the object.
fieldHelp.required: Properties that are mandatory when creating a record.
fieldHelp.secondary: Secondary properties shown in the object's views.
fieldHelp.searchable: Properties that records can be searched by.
fieldHelp.associations: Other objects this object can be associated with.

customObjects.archiveConfirmBody: Archiving requires first deleting the object's records and properties in HubSpot. Continue?
customObjects.deleteDraftBody: This deletes the object draft. This action cannot be undone. Continue?

forms.title: CRM / Forms
forms.empty: No forms yet. Sync with HubSpot or create one.
forms.noResults: No results with the current filters.

panel.title: Form detail
panel.noCoverage: Link an origin to see coverage.

editWizard.title: Edit form "{{name}}"
editWizard.name: Form name
editWizard.label: Label
editWizard.noSubscriptions: No Email subscription types in the portal.

fieldHelp.name: Name of the form in HubSpot.
fieldHelp.fieldName: Internal name of the field (associated HubSpot property).
fieldHelp.label: Visible text of the field's label.
fieldHelp.fieldType: Field type (text, dropdown, date, checkbox, etc.).
fieldHelp.hidden: Hides the field in the form (it is submitted but not shown).
fieldHelp.consentType: Legal basis for processing the form's data (no consent, legitimate interest, or explicit consent).
fieldHelp.submitButton: Text of the button the user submits the form with.
fieldHelp.privacyText: Legal privacy text shown in the form.
fieldHelp.consentToProcessText: Text of the consent to process the data.
fieldHelp.communicationConsentText: Text of the consent to receive communications.
fieldHelp.subscriptionType: Email subscription type associated with the communication checkbox.

fieldHelp.object: HubSpot object the origin to be linked belongs to.
fieldHelp.origins: Origins linked to the form to evaluate field coverage.

wizard.title: Create form
wizard.name: Form name
wizard.noFields: Pick an object and one or more origins to preselect fields.
wizard.label: Label

fieldHelp.name: Name of the form to identify it in HubSpot.
fieldHelp.object: HubSpot object the form creates or updates records on.
fieldHelp.origins: Origins that preselect the form's expected fields.
fieldHelp.label: Visible text of the field's label for the user.
fieldHelp.fieldType: Field type (text, dropdown, date, checkbox, etc.).
fieldHelp.required: Marks the field as mandatory to submit the form.
fieldHelp.hidden: Hides the field in the form (it is submitted but not shown).

forms.discardBody: This discards the form's pending change. Continue?

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.onboardingIntro: Set up the connectors to start working.
dashboard.error: Could not load the project status.

crm.title: CRM
crm.notConnected: Connect HubSpot to see your CRM data.
crm.error: Could not load the CRM status.

common.loadError: The information could not be loaded.
